title: Introducing FORTNIGHT HISTORY TEST so you don't havd to lick butthole at school anymore

enrichment.summary: Kyle introduces his 'Fortnite History Test,' a bi-weekly self-graded quiz where you hit a random number generator to get a decade and have one minute to recall historical events from it, repeated 60 times. He argues this system could replace formal schooling entirely, allowing kids to drop out after 6th grade, self-educate through history study and physical exercise, and eventually qualify for graduate programs without years of tuition. He frames it as an alternative to what he sees as corrupt, money-extracting academia, and envisions billionaires funding 'bike schoolers' who study history while cycling.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Corrupt, money-extracting scam that makes people dumb rather than educated
The superior alternative to school — rigorous, self-graded, exposes dishonesty, teaches more than years of academia
More valuable than being graded by teachers because it exposes your own weaknesses and builds real intelligence
Best way to study history is while exercising — cycling, rowing, swimming — listening to history books
Dangerously ignorant of history, proof that dumb leaders cause societal collapse
Billionaires should pay self-educated bike schoolers $50k/year instead of funding schools
Money wasted on schools should fund tree-planting robots to restore lakes, rivers, and fight climate change
Credential races (youngest graduate, most degrees) are nepotism and payoffs, not merit

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle did his first bi-weekly history test last night and graded himself today.

claims.subject: bi-weekly history test
claims.claim: The bi-weekly history test is a way to avoid traditional schooling by excelling in the test, which can lead to acceptance into graduate programs without years of preamble.

claims.subject: education
claims.claim: Education today is about elite overproduction, where the focus is on graduating as young as possible, often fueled by nepotism and money.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle graduated with an English degree at 23 after two summer sessions and was kicked out of the education department.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle attended Fort Lewis College in Durango and was rejected from CU Boulder because he dropped out of chemistry.

claims.subject: bi-weekly history test
claims.claim: The bi-weekly history test involves using a random number generator to select decades and recalling events from those decades within a minute.

claims.subject: bi-weekly history test
claims.claim: The bi-weekly history test requires precise knowledge of events, as close calls or rounding up decades do not count.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle scored 154 on his first bi-weekly history test, mentioning 154 things that happened across random decades.

claims.subject: bi-weekly history test
claims.claim: The bi-weekly history test exposes personal knowledge and honesty, as participants must grade themselves accurately.

claims.subject: bi-weekly history test
claims.claim: The bi-weekly history test can be done with ChatGPT for quick verification and explanation of historical events.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to train his mind by exercising while listening to history books and recordings of different decades.

claims.subject: bi-weekly history test
claims.claim: The bi-weekly history test can be taken every two weeks, with different versions focusing on AD, BC, ancient history, and the 20th century.

claims.subject: education
claims.claim: Kyle believes that shutting down schools and redirecting funds to robots planting trees could solve environmental issues like global warming.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: Donald Trump thinks Colorado borders Mexico and that the English bombed airports during the American Revolution.

claims.subject: bi-weekly history test
claims.claim: The bi-weekly history test could expose leaders like Donald Trump for their lack of historical knowledge.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes that people who excel at the bi-weekly history test should be paid $50,000 a year to focus on learning and fitness.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle wants to be sponsored by billionaires to fund his bi-weekly history test and bike training, allowing him to pay off his HELOC loan and buy new bikes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to ask Elon Musk for sponsorship, calling the bi-weekly history test the 'X test' and himself the 'X racer.'

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle will combine the bi-weekly history test with book reviews and physical activities like cycling, swimming, and rowing.

claims.subject: bi-weekly history test
claims.claim: The bi-weekly history test is the beginning of a new world where people teach themselves and schools are shut down.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle encourages people to ask anyone, including college presidents and mayors, to take the bi-weekly history test, backed by the authority of bike school.
